title: Jerusalem 14-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Jerusalem 14-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Jerusalem, Israel: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Tower of David Museum and Israel Museum. Prices from $2504 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Jerusalem, Israel — Hot-summer Mediterranean climate with a hill-city twist — warm, dry summers (highs around 28-29°C) and cool, sometimes rainy winters (highs around 12°C, occasional light snow). April-May and September-October, for mild temperatures ideal for walking the Old City. This itinerary blends Tower of David Museum, Israel Museum, Western Wall with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Tower of David Museum, Israel Museum, Western Wall
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Jerusalem
itinerary.description: Land at Ben Gurion Airport (nearest, ~45 minutes away) (TLV) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Tower of David Museum — Citadel museum tracing Jerusalem's history, with a night sound-and-light show. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: Israel Museum & Yad Vashem
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Israel Museum — Home to the Dead Sea Scrolls and the Shrine of the Book. In the afternoon, continue to Yad Vashem — Holocaust memorial and museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: City of David archaeological site & Western Wall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to City of David archaeological site — Excavated ruins beneath the Old City, including ancient water tunnels. In the afternoon, continue to Western Wall — Judaism's holiest accessible prayer site, part of the Second Temple retaining wall. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bethlehem
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bethlehem, a popular side trip from Jerusalem. Return to Jerusalem in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Ramparts Walk atop the Old City walls — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Ramparts Walk atop the Old City walls

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: Old City of Jerusalem & Church of the Holy Sepulchre
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Old City of Jerusalem — Walled historic center split into Jewish, Christian, Muslim, and Armenian quarters. In the afternoon, continue to Church of the Holy Sepulchre — Site venerated as Jesus's crucifixion and burial location. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Dead Sea and Masada
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Dead Sea and Masada, a popular side trip from Jerusalem. Return to Jerusalem in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Dead Sea and Masada

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: Mount of Olives viewpoint & Mahane Yehuda Market
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mount of Olives viewpoint — Panoramic view over the Old City and Temple Mount. In the afternoon, continue to Mahane Yehuda Market — Lively covered market by day, bar scene by night. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Hezekiah's Tunnel wade-through — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Tel Aviv (45 minutes)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Tel Aviv (45 minutes), a popular side trip from Jerusalem. Return to Jerusalem in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Tel Aviv (45 minutes)

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: Tower of David Museum & Israel Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tower of David Museum — Citadel museum tracing Jerusalem's history, with a night sound-and-light show. In the afternoon, continue to Israel Museum — Home to the Dead Sea Scrolls and the Shrine of the Book. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Jerusalem: Yad Vashem & City of David archaeological site
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Yad Vashem — Holocaust memorial and museum. In the afternoon, continue to City of David archaeological site — Excavated ruins beneath the Old City, including ancient water tunnels. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Ein Kerem village — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Western Wall or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to TLV for your departure flight.

travelTips: Visit the Western Wall and Old City early morning for a quieter, more contemplative experience.
Book the City of David and Hezekiah's Tunnel in advance and bring water shoes for the wet tunnel route.
Public transport largely pauses for Shabbat (Friday evening-Saturday evening) — plan accordingly.
Allocate at least two full days for the Old City alone, given its density of sites.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing (shoulders and knees covered) for religious sites
A head covering for men at the Western Wall and women at some sites
Comfortable, sturdy shoes for the Old City's uneven stone streets and stairs
thingsToDo: Wading through Hezekiah's Tunnel (City of David)
Ramparts Walk along the Old City walls
thingsToAvoid: Dressing immodestly at religious sites — cover-ups are sometimes provided but not guaranteed.
Public displays of affection near religious sites.
Booking travel without checking current regional advisories, given the security situation can change.
Rushing the Old City — its four quarters each reward slow, unhurried exploration.
topRestaurants: Yudale — Israeli tapas, Mahane Yehuda Market ($$)
Rooftop Restaurant Notre Dame — Mediterranean with Old City views ($$$)
instagramSpots: Old City rooftops from the Ramparts Walk
hiddenGems: Ramparts Walk atop the Old City walls
adventureActivities: Wading through Hezekiah's Tunnel (City of David)
Ramparts Walk along the Old City walls
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the King David Hotel
Private Dead Sea day trip with a driver
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset from the Mount of Olives
Private candlelit dinner overlooking the Old City walls
A quiet walk through Ein Kerem village
budgetExperiences: Free entry to the Western Wall and Old City
Mahane Yehuda Market wander and street food
localEtiquette: Dress modestly for religious sites — shoulders and knees covered; some sites require head coverings.
Respect the customs of each quarter (Jewish, Christian, Muslim, Armenian) within the Old City.
Shabbat (Friday evening to Saturday evening) brings much of the city, especially West Jerusalem, to a quiet standstill.
Photography is restricted in some religious areas — look for posted signage.
localTransportGuide: The Old City is entirely pedestrian and best explored on foot, though the terrain is hilly with many stairs. The Jerusalem Light Rail and buses connect the newer parts of the city; taxis and rideshare apps are widely used, especially since public transit pauses for Shabbat.
bestTimeToVisit: April-May and September-October, for mild temperatures ideal for walking the Old City.
weather: Hot-summer Mediterranean climate with a hill-city twist — warm, dry summers (highs around 28-29°C) and cool, sometimes rainy winters (highs around 12°C, occasional light snow).
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need an e-visa arranged in advance. Check current entry requirements and regional travel advisories before booking, as conditions can change.
averageFlightTime: ~9.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Jerusalem?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need an e-visa arranged in advance. Check current entry requirements and regional travel advisories before booking, as conditions can change.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Jerusalem?
faqs.answer: April-May and September-October, for mild temperatures ideal for walking the Old City. Hot-summer Mediterranean climate with a hill-city twist — warm, dry summers (highs around 28-29°C) and cool, sometimes rainy winters (highs around 12°C, occasional light snow).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Jerusalem safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Jerusalem is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Jerusalem?
faqs.answer: The local currency is ILS. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.
